title: BTNYC Configuration Schema
description: Schema for the btnyc.json configuration file – single source of truth for pricing, services, and UI.

$schema.description: v9.1: SSOT linkage back to btnyc_schema.json so validators/editors can resolve the schema without being told the path out-of-band.

$id.description: v9.1: canonical published URL of this data file (GitHub Pages).

version.description: Content/release counter. Bumps on any meaningful edit (new services, retagged smart_tags, new modules). Distinct from schema_version — see that field's description.

schema_version.description: v9.3: structural compatibility counter. Only bumps when the JSON's SHAPE changes in a way old code would misread (renamed/removed keys, new required fields, changed nesting) — not on ordinary content edits. btnyc.py's MIGRATIONS dict keys off this field to decide whether a migration preview is needed on load. Optional for backward compatibility with files saved before this field existed; treated as the last-known-compatible version when absent.

divergence_resolution.description: v9.5: 'Fork in the Road' -- PROJECT_GOALS §9/10 (the Confidence-Friction Seesaw's Diagnostic Divergence). Shared, global business terms for the choice offered when a route hits the diagnostic dead end (checkout_state resolves to 'diagnostic') AND the resolved entity has real, authored remote_deep_dive_modules (see services[].remote_deep_dive_modules / dynamic_services[*].remote_deep_dive_modules). Optional and additive: entities without remote_deep_dive_modules are completely unaffected and keep today's existing single-path diagnostic behavior.

enabled.description: Global kill switch, matching the surcharges.dispatch_fee_enabled convention. Defaults to true (treated as enabled unless explicitly set false).

diagnostic_fee.description: Flat fee for Path B (On-Site Pro). Sourced directly from PROJECT_GOALS' own worked example ($85) -- not an independently-derived business figure. Business decision on final value pending, same status as surcharges.dispatch_fee_apply_min_labor.

credit_policy.description: v9.5.1: originally specified as a short, machine-readable key (e.g. 'full_credit_if_repair_accepted'); the real, current authored data instead puts a full, real, customer-facing sentence directly here (e.g. 'If you book the on-site diagnostic, the $85 fee is fully credited toward any subsequent repair work.') and does not populate credit_policy_note separately. Both computeUnifiedQuote's divergenceCreditNote and buildDivergenceResolutionHtml's rendered card read credit_policy_note first, falling back to this field -- so either a short-key style (paired with credit_policy_note for the real customer copy) or this single-field prose style works correctly. sqAddToCart also records this value on the cart entry when a customer books via the On-Site Pro path (disclosure/record-keeping; there is no automated credit-at-invoicing mechanism yet).

credit_policy_note.description: Optional, dedicated customer-facing copy explaining the credit policy, shown on the On-Site Pro option card and used as computeUnifiedQuote's divergenceCreditNote -- checked BEFORE credit_policy (see that field's description) so a short machine-readable credit_policy key can still be paired with real customer prose here if that two-field shape is preferred going forward. Not populated in the current real data (credit_policy carries the full sentence directly instead); kept as the preferred field when present for exactly that reason.

hub_heading.description: Heading text for the two-option 'Information Resolution Hub' card.

per_unit_minutes.description: Extra minutes added per unit beyond the first (qty-aware, same role as tile_repair_formula.minutes_per_tile). Lets a 4-drawer job scale by actual per-drawer effort instead of the generic global +30min/unit padding.

issue_type_fees.description: Keyed by the exact client_response label string from the 'issue' intake_module (e.g. 'Broken piece (leg, frame, etc.)'). Each entry adds a flat fee and/or minutes on top of base_minutes — same lookup pattern as tile_repair_formula's grout_repair/water_damage checks, generalized to any label rather than a hardcoded Yes/No.

nlp_clause_boundaries.description: Relative pronouns / clause-introducing words (that, which, who, whose, where) that terminate object-noun extraction, same role as nlp_stop_preps but for relative clauses rather than prepositional phrases. Without this, a sentence like 'the medallion THAT has separated' kept walking past 'that' into the clause that describes the object instead of stopping at the object itself, since nlp_stop_words only skips these words rather than treating them as a boundary.

effects.description: Deprecated v9.4 -- smart_tags no longer carry independent fee/complexity effects directly. Use escalate_complexity for complexity-tier effects; per-answer fees now live on the intake_module's own client_response via modifier_ref.

escalate_complexity.description: v9.4: replaces effects.complexity_override. Ratchets the resolved complexity tier up (never down) when this tag is active and there is real time-based headroom to escalate into.

applicable_group_ids.description: Optional further narrowing WITHIN an already-matching category (see tagValidForCategory in qr.html). When present on a tag whose applicable_categories spans multiple categories, must include the correct group id(s) from EVERY one of those categories — group ids are not unique across categories, so an incomplete list silently excludes the tag from categories it's still meant to apply to.

typical_location.description: v9.5: optional. When a group's items are almost always found in one room (e.g. a stove is always in the kitchen), this lets the SmartQuote guided builder skip its generic 'Where in the home?' step instead of asking a question with an obvious, self-evident answer. Must match one of BLD_LOCATIONS' real `value` strings in qr.html (e.g. 'kitchen', 'bathroom') -- left unset for genuinely ambiguous groups (e.g. furniture, wall-mounted items, which could be in any room).

type.description: v9.4: renamed from pricing_type as part of the financial_engine simplification. Values include flat_rate, hourly, formula, assembly_formula.

pricing_type.description: Deprecated v9.4 -- superseded by 'type'. Retained optionally for backward compatibility with pre-v9.4 files.

base_materials.description: Deprecated v9.4 -- materials are now costed via required_materials/materials_catalog and shown separately as route.materialsEstimate, never folded into base price.

formula_ref.description: v9.4: authoritative reference into pricing_formulas for formula-priced services. Replaces reading the formula id off the sibling top-level pricing_engine field.

pricing_archetype.description: Deprecated v9.4 -- pricing archetype is now derived from type + formula_ref (see compiled.pricing_index) rather than authored directly on the entity.

remote_deep_dive_modules.description: v9.5: real intake_modules keys (must exist in intake_modules) that Path A ('Remote Deep Dive') of the divergence_resolution Fork appends to this service's normal intake_chain when the customer opts into answering more, deeper questions instead of booking an on-site diagnostic visit (Path B). Only meaningful when this service's checkout_state resolves to 'diagnostic' -- see global_rules.divergence_resolution. Deliberately NOT authored for most diagnostic-natured services yet (writing a genuinely correct, non-fabricated deep-dive question set per service/appliance type needs real domain input) -- absent or empty means this service keeps today's existing single-path diagnostic behavior unchanged, exactly like a service with no divergence_resolution config at all.

per_unit_answers_vary.description: v9.5: when true, this service's intake answers genuinely describe ONE specific unit (e.g. a particular door's size/style/frame-removal status), not a generalizable per-unit fact that scales safely with quantity. Such a service should NOT show the qty stepper at all -- a customer needing this work done on two structurally different units (e.g. a front door vs. a bathroom door, each with its own size, material, and removal status) should book two SEPARATE service requests, each with its own correct, specific answers, rather than one qty=2 entry that can only carry a single shared set of answers and would silently misattribute one unit's specifics to both. Distinct from a real, generalizable quantity question (e.g. 'how many light bulbs' -- the answer genuinely is the same fact applied N times).

pricing_archetype.description: Deprecated v9.4 -- see services[].financial_engine.pricing_archetype for the same deprecation note.

remote_deep_dive_modules.description: v9.5: same real mechanism as services[].remote_deep_dive_modules (see that field's description) -- included here so the 24 real 'Diagnostic' dynamic_services entries can eventually carry their own, correctly-authored deep-dive question sets. Deliberately unauthored on all of them today -- a genuinely real, per-category (appliances/plumbing/tech) content-authoring task, not a mechanism gap.

effects.description: Deprecated v9.4, fully retired v9.6 -- superseded by modifier_ref and the top-level override fields below. Zero real client_response entries use this anymore (verified via exhaustive scan); kept in the schema only for historical-file compatibility.

modifier_ref.description: v9.4: authoritative reference into global_rules.modifiers for this answer's fee/minutes. Replaces inline effects.{fee,minutes}. As of v9.6 this is the only place fee/minutes are ever authored.

complexity_override.description: v9.4: top-level on the response, not nested under effects.

checkout_state_override.description: v9.4: top-level on the response, not nested under effects.

formula_override.description: v9.6: top-level on the response (was nested under effects pre-v9.6). References a pricing_formulas key -- when this answer is selected, computeUnifiedQuote routes pricing through the named formula instead of the entity's default engine.

qty_value.description: v9.6: top-level on the response (was nested under effects pre-v9.6). Explicit numeric quantity this label represents (e.g. label '6 or more' -> qty_value 6), read instead of parsing the label text directly.

location_hints.description: v9.5: list of negation_library.nlp_location_words values for which THIS specific answer is the strongly likely/default one (e.g. a 'front door' implies solid-core; a 'bedroom'/'hallway' door implies hollow-core). Genuinely ambiguous locations are simply omitted from every answer's location_hints for this module (e.g. a 'guest bedroom' vs 'bathroom' both plausibly mean a toilet -- neither should claim it confidently). When S._location matches exactly one answer's location_hints, that answer is pre-selected (still changeable by the customer, never silently applied without being shown). This generalizes beyond doors: any intake question whose correct answer correlates strongly with WHERE the job is should use this, since the location chip otherwise sits unused as filler/notes despite carrying real, derivable intent.

flat_fee.description: Flat per-item assembly fee in dollars, typically computed as (minutes / 60) * shop hourly rate and rounded to the nearest dollar. Replaces the legacy, never-consumed upcharge_percent field.

workflow.description: Phase 0 of the Orchestrator Overhaul. The active rulebook for routing, intake-question composition, confidence gating, and pricing -- consulted by executeWorkflow(context, db) for every real entry path. See workflow._note for full context.

required_fields.description: Root-level (or near-root) node names that must exist for the workflow to execute at all. Phase 5's pre-compile audit fails the build if any of these is missing.

resolution_thresholds.description: The two real confidence thresholds governing free-text entity resolution -- previously hardcoded in two separate places, never read from the SSOT.

invariants.description: The Judicial Rulebook (Master Blueprint Phase 4). Unbendable laws a ResolvedRoute must satisfy -- the RouteValidator's actual rulebook, not hardcoded JS conditionals. Each entry's `check` is evaluated generically by evaluateInvariant() in qr.html against a real ResolvedRoute.

check.description: A small, generic check expression. type determines which other fields are required: exists/non_empty_array/non_negative (field); enum (field, allowed); conditional_exists (if_field, if_value, then_field); conditional_not_equal (if_field, if_values, then_field, forbidden_value); lte (field_a, field_b).

compiled.description: v8: real, compiler-generated, derived data (pricing_index, component_index, action_index, symptom_index, service_index, candidate_matrix) -- additive output of btnyc_v8_compiler.py, never hand-authored. Internal shape is the compiler's own, separately-tested responsibility (see test_harness/verify_btnyc_v8_compiler.py); deliberately left permissive here since it legitimately evolves as the compiler does, unlike the rest of this schema's hand-authored, strictly-specified sections.

routing_archetypes.description: v8: real, compiler-generated classification of each group's routing strategy (component_first/symptom_first/undetermined), derived from real_components/real_symptoms counts. Was 'group_archetypes' through v7; renamed to genuinely separate this concept from pricing_archetypes, since 'archetype' had become overloaded across two distinct, real ideas. Additive output of btnyc_v8_compiler.py.

pricing_archetypes.description: v8: real, centrally-defined list of the 5 real, schema-confirmed pricing archetypes (see services[].financial_engine.pricing_archetype's own enum) with live-computed current assignment counts. Additive output of btnyc_v8_compiler.py.

_compiler_metadata.description: v8: real, compiler-generated metadata (version, source file, any real, honest exclusion lists) -- additive output of btnyc_v8_compiler.py.

_validation.description: v8: real, compiler-generated reference-validation results (e.g. unknown intake-module/material references) -- additive output of btnyc_v8_compiler.py.

_additive_diff.description: v8: real, compiler-generated proof that every original, hand-authored top-level key survived the compilation byte-for-byte unchanged -- additive output of btnyc_v8_compiler.py.

_schema_validation.description: v8: real, new, self-referential schema-validation result -- the compiler's own check of whether ITS OWN complete output (including this very key) validates against this schema. Added directly in response to a real, confirmed gap: every prior version's reference checks never actually ran a real JSON Schema validator. Additive output of btnyc_v8_compiler.py.

checkout_state_override.description: v9.3 architecture (backlog — see CHANGELOG): lets a SPECIFIC answer escalate checkout_state (e.g. to 'diagnostic') independently of the service's default, instead of the service-wide flag applying to every answer uniformly regardless of which one was actually chosen. Must be a key in checkout_states. Most-restrictive-wins across all answered questions, same ratchet pattern as complexity_override. Not yet authored on any client_response — this field is schema-ready but the actual per-answer authoring work for the 16 currently-always-diagnostic services is deferred.

formula_override.description: Lets a SPECIFIC answer set/override which pricing_formulas entry applyPricingFormula should use, independently of any upstream NLP keyword match. Closes a real gap found during the backlog item A investigation: dynamic_rule previously only ever lived on intent_mappings.objects (set once, before the customer answers any real question), meaning a customer reaching the same tile/drywall-specific branch via a DIFFERENT entry path (e.g. tapping a generic Other tile, then answering surface_type directly) never triggered the formula at all, even though they answered the exact same question. Must be a key in pricing_formulas. First real answer to set this wins (does not need a ratchet/most-restrictive pattern like checkout_state_override, since at most one surface_type answer can ever be selected).

intake_chain_step.description: v9.1: shared by services.intake_chain and dynamic_services.intake_chain so the two never drift. 'params' lets a step override a parameterized intake_module (e.g. item_count_template) with per-service values (item_noun, client_response bands) without duplicating the module definition in intake_modules.

params.description: Per-step override values for a parameterized intake_module (e.g. item_count_template). Common keys: item_noun (string substituted into the module's {item_noun} placeholder), client_response (array, same shape as intake_modules[*].client_response, overrides the template's generic bands with this service's exact fee/minute values), question_override (string, exact original question text as a fallback if noun substitution reads awkwardly).

effects.description: Deprecated v9.4, fully retired v9.6 -- superseded by modifier_ref and the top-level override fields below.

modifier_ref.description: v9.4: authoritative reference into global_rules.modifiers. As of v9.6, per-service quantity-tier fees (e.g. item_count_template overrides) are each given a uniquely-named modifier here rather than an inline fee, so every fee in the SSOT resolves through the same single mechanism.

formula_override.description: v9.6: top-level (was nested under effects pre-v9.6).

qty_value.description: v9.6: top-level (was nested under effects pre-v9.6).

booking_context.description: Phase 1 of the Orchestrator Overhaul. The single, strict shape every real entry path (Catalog browse, Other-tile tap, free-text SmartQuote) must produce before calling executeWorkflow. No UI element may make a routing or pricing decision -- it may only populate these fields from what the user directly did (tapped, typed) or what a real NLP/lookup function directly returned. This is documentation of the JS-side BookingContext factory's exact output shape, not data consumed by qr.html at runtime -- it exists here so the contract is auditable from the SSOT side, matching this project's broader principle that the JSON should describe the process, not just hold inert data.

entry.description: Which of the 3 real UI entry points produced this context. Drives nothing on its own -- every downstream workflow step treats all 3 identically once a BookingContext exists. Its only purpose is trace logging (Phase 7) and shadow-mode diffing (Phase 5.5).

selectedServiceId.description: Set when entry='catalog' (a named service was tapped directly) or when entry='free_text' resolved to a specific recommendedSku at high confidence. Null otherwise.

selectedGroupId.description: Set when entry='other_tile' (a synthetic 'Other' tile for a known category+group was tapped) or when free-text resolved to a group via intent_mappings.objects without a confident enough match for a specific service.

rawText.description: The customer's literal typed text. Only ever set when entry='free_text'. Never populated or synthesized for catalog/other_tile entries -- a directly-tapped service has no typed text behind it, and treating default_tags or other inherent service facts as if they came from typed text was a confirmed real bug this session (the 'We understood: ...' banner falsely firing for a directly-tapped service).

nlpIntent.description: The raw, unmodified return value of detectIntentNLP(rawText) when entry='free_text'. Carries category, stype, group, base, recommendedSku, _matchConfidence, dynamic_rule, and the contextual-override fields (_ctxFee/_ctxMin/_ctxTags) exactly as detectIntentNLP produces them -- never re-derived or guessed by the entry-point collector itself.

extractedObject.description: The literal noun phrase extractObject() pulled from rawText (e.g. 'fridge', 'drawer pulls'). Distinct from selectedServiceId/selectedGroupId -- this is raw extraction, resolve_entity (workflow step) is what turns it into a real entity.

extractedLocation.description: The literal location word extractLocation() pulled from rawText, matched against negation_library.nlp_location_words. Consumed by the workflow's apply_location_hints step -- never used to pre-fill an answer by the entry-point collector itself, since that decision (exactly-one-match-or-stay-silent) belongs to the orchestrator, not the UI.

manuallyToggledTagIds.description: Tags the user explicitly tapped as a chip. Distinct from NLP-detected tags and from a service's inherent default_tags -- see inherentTagIds below, the same real distinction the session's 'We understood' banner fix established.

uncoveredServiceTypes.description: Set when entry='other_tile'. The list of service_types not yet covered by a named service for this group (carried from the tile's own uncovered_service_types). Consumed by workflow.intake_bypass_rules: when this array has exactly one entry, the action/type-selection step is unambiguous and can be skipped.
